title: Big questions in modern and contemporary art
topic_path: Big questions in modern and contemporary art

assessment_items.question_content:
Damien Hirst's *The Physical Impossibility of Death in the Mind of Someone Living* (1991) is . . .

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d8c05fa9908788d2e558b97eb4e32a8c.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: There are many ways to look at Hirst's shark.

assessment_items.question_content:
All Contemporary artists work in a similar style, with similar themes and cultural concerns.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Contemporary artists engage with a wide variety of styles, media, themes, and cultural concerns.

assessment_items.question_content:
Pop artists, like Andy Warhol, were interested in representing . . .

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: everyday objects and images from popular culture.
high minded ideas like justice and wisdom.
the extremes of emotions that people feel in extreme circumstances.
scenes from the Bible that reinforced Christian beliefs.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jackson Pollock was influenced by . . .

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: There were numerous influences on Jackson Pollock's work.

assessment_items.question_content:
John Everett Millais' painting *Ophelia* (1851-52) reveals . . .

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0ebbe97eed403f03cc8fa6988a3e8ea5.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The painting reveals a lot about the time and place in which it was made

choices.content: that a tremendous amount of natural detail was carefully observed by the artist.
that art in the nineteenth century was focused on creating convincing illusions, using paint to portray things we recognize.
that Pre-Raphaelite artists were focused on returning to a style based on direct observation that they believed had been lost after the Renaissance.

assessment_items.question_content:
Context is important in understanding many works of art, but context is not important in understanding abstract art.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Understanding the place and time in which a work of art was made is helpful in figuring out what the artist was trying to achieve in their art.
The Abstract Expressionists were skilled artists who experimented with paint and meaning in new ways.

choices.content: shows that the artist was focused on the paint and did not use this painting to create an illusion of a thing in the world.
shows that Abstract Expressionists hated nature.
shows that Abstract Expressionists did not have the skills to represent things that exist in real life.
shows that you can't create meaningful art without recognizable objects, places, and people.
